title: Source
description: The canonical projected shape of a source: what source.discovered, .identity.resolved, .rights.assessed, .fetch.observed, .archive.requested and .archive.confirmed each contribute a slice of (SPEC.md §4.2). No single event carries this whole shape — the projector folds a source's events into this record; a batch payload for one of those event types carries only the fields it observed.

discovered_via.description: Free text: how this source entered the ledger (paste, file drop, agent search, candidate pool seed, ...).

identity.description: source.identity.resolved — is this the same work as one already in the ledger?

resolution_basis.description: What established identity/duplication — a matching content hash, an ISBN, a DOI, a human judgment.

rights.description: source.rights.assessed — never a runtime guess; recorded once, evented, re-assessable only via a new event.

basis.description: Why this status: a license URI, a jurisdiction's term length, a stated fair-use rationale.

fetch.description: source.fetch.observed — what was actually retrieved, content-addressed so a later re-fetch's drift is detectable.

archive.description: source.archive.requested / .confirmed — an independent witness to the fetched bytes (custody vs. attestation are deliberately separate: you hold the bytes, an archive holds a witness to them).
